progress.TG_P11_0: PASS_BASELINE_PINNED (TG-P10 repro still fails; classifier EMITTER_BLOCKED; owned default unchanged)
progress.TG_P11_1: PASS_INVARIANT_TEST_READY (minimal attention-free microgate extra/qk_tg_p11_reduce_upcast_microgate.py reproduces the invariant: a scalar-REG END-accumulator + a foldable contiguous load in the reduce body mis-lowers)
progress.TG_P11_2: BLOCKED_LOWERING_STILL_WRONG (a correct, generic, safe fix could not be landed in-session)

refined_root_cause.mechanism: The hand flash/gemv kernels express reductions with a MANUAL END/AFTER accumulator idiom (acc.set(acc.after(s)+v, end=s)), NOT Ops.REDUCE. So reduce_to_acc / horizontal_reduce (which correctly folds upcast reduce lanes) never runs on them. When a contiguous load in the reduce body is folded to float4 (coalesced), the scalar-REG accumulate `acc + float4` broadcasts acc to a vector and emits an invalid `make_float4(acc,acc,acc,acc) = make_float4(acc+v0,..,acc+v3)` store; REG_STORE_DEVEC aliases the 4 lanes into slot 0 -> NaN.
refined_root_cause.why_shipped_kernels_compile: gmax uses a STRIDED load (not folded -> stays scalar); flash_state_combine's inline fexp blocks the fold. Verified: 0 invalid stores in the shipped combine, >0 in every fexp-free / weight-sharing shape and in the minimal microgate.
refined_root_cause.confirmed: the manual accumulator produces END=2/AFTER=4/STORE=3, Ops.REDUCE=0 -> bypasses the horizontal-reduce path entirely.

candidate_fixes.where: a gated (REDUCE_ACC_UPCAST_FIX) devectorizer PatternMatcher rule matching STORE(INDEX(REG_scalar), ALU(op, broadcast(acc), contrib_vec)) -> STORE(INDEX(REG_scalar), op(acc, horizontal_reduce(contrib_vec, op)))
candidate_fixes.risk: op-specific correctness (max = plain HREDUCE; add = HREDUCE - (n-1)*acc); fragile broadcast/contrib detection in the post-devectorize graph

candidate_fixes.approach: prevent the load-fold in a scalar-REG reduce body
candidate_fixes.where: gate load_store_folding to skip a contiguous load whose consumer is a scalar-REG END-accumulator, keeping the reduce scalar (like gmax)
candidate_fixes.risk: perf regression (scalar loads) for legit cases; must be measured to confirm the combine is still faster than the shipped fexp path

why_not_landed: both fixes touch core reduce/devectorize lowering used by EVERY kernel (Q4_K G3 GEMV, Q6_K coop, prefill GEMM, all attention tiles). A subtle regression would silently corrupt outputs model-wide. Landing either responsibly needs reliable UOp-graph pattern detection (which requires deeper interception than achieved here) plus a full model-wide token-parity + W==D regression pass -- a dedicated compiler-engineering effort beyond a single session's safe scope.
distance_to_north_star: one gated core fix (horizontal-reduce the manual END-accumulator under load-fold, op-specific) + model-wide regression validation. Then the TG-P9 live-split tile + the unblocked fexp-free combine run TG-P11.5/6/7 and should clear >=98% at ctx512/4096.
reopen: land one of the two candidate fixes behind REDUCE_ACC_UPCAST_FIX with the route regression ladder green, re-emit reg_scalar_lowering.v1 (BoltBeam flips REACHABLE), then TG-P11.5/6/7.
